notes:
**Fixes & polish**

- **Edits made just before leaving a zoomed view can no longer be lost.** Typing
  in a zoomed block and immediately navigating away — Back, a breadcrumb,
  keyboard shortcuts, switching or closing tabs — now always saves what you
  wrote before the view changes, on every navigation path. (PRD Addendum 20 §T:
  zoom is "a view, never a data change.")
- **Sync never throws your changes away.** A synced change this version of the
  app can't read yet (for example, written by a newer device) is now safely
  *parked* and retried after you update, instead of being skipped permanently.
  (PRD Addendum 16; "park, don't drop.")
- **Notes containing content from a newer app version now load safely.** Blocks
  this version doesn't understand appear as read-only placeholders that preserve
  the original content exactly — previously such a note could load empty, and
  saving it could overwrite the real content, including on your other devices.
  (PRD Addendum 16 §compatibility.)
- **Sync can no longer get permanently stuck.** A change stamped with a clock
  too far in the future (a device with a wrong clock) used to wedge syncing
  forever; it now recovers automatically. And rotating your encryption key from
  two devices at the same time can no longer lose a key.
  (PRD Addendum 16.)
- **The floating toolbar no longer covers a selected image or embed.**

**New**

- **Browse got real data.** Word counts, attachment indicators, and a
  "recently opened" sort are now backed by live data, and unlinked mentions
  have a home in the references panel. (PRD Addendum 22 §8.9, Group E.)

**Under the hood**

- The sync engine moved into its own crate with cross-version compatibility
  fixtures, wire versioning (clients now identify their platform and version),
  a server-side account-deletion endpoint, and hardened cursor handling.
  (PRD Addendum 16, Track D.)
- Optional OS-keychain custody for sync keys on desktop (dark-launched; off by
  default outside the dev channel). (ADR-0012.)
- Foundations for the iOS and Android apps now live in this repository: the
  mobile FFI core, the mobile web bundle, and both native shells. Invisible to
  the desktop app.
